basics.name: Richard Hendricks
basics.label: Software engineer and compression enthusiast
basics.summary: Software engineer and founder focused on compression, distributed systems, and building products out of improbable technical breakthroughs. Most comfortable close to the code, but experienced in the strange overlap between engineering, product direction, fundraising, and keeping a startup alive long enough for the architecture to matter.

work.name: Pied Piper
work.summary: Founded a software company built around a novel compression algorithm and later expanded the product direction into decentralized storage, media processing, and developer infrastructure.
highlights: Designed and implemented the original compression engine that became the technical foundation of the company.
Led early product decisions across compression, distributed storage, and developer-facing tooling.
Built and managed a small engineering team through multiple product pivots, infrastructure rewrites, funding cycles, and launch constraints.
Worked across backend services, systems architecture, performance optimization, and product prototyping.
Represented the company in investor meetings, technical demos, launch events, and customer conversations while continuing to contribute to core engineering work.

work.name: Hooli
work.summary: Worked on large-scale software systems inside a major technology company while developing independent research into compression algorithms.
highlights: Contributed to internal engineering tools and platform services used by large product teams.
Gained experience working in a highly scaled engineering organization with formal review, deployment, and operational processes.
Developed experimental compression work outside core product responsibilities, eventually leading to the first Pied Piper prototype.
Learned several lessons about what not to optimize for when building software, teams, and companies.

work.name: Aviato Incubator
work.summary: Built early versions of Pied Piper from a startup incubator environment alongside other founders, engineers, and product experiments.
highlights: Turned a prototype compression algorithm into a demonstrable product concept.
Prepared technical demos, early architecture plans, and investor-facing materials.
Collaborated with other founders and advisors on market positioning, product direction, and company formation.
Discovered that the hardest part of a startup is not always the algorithm.

work.name: Independent Projects
work.summary: Built small tools, prototypes, and experiments around media processing, peer-to-peer systems, and data compression.
highlights: Implemented early compression experiments in C, Python, and JavaScript.
Built prototypes for music analysis, file deduplication, and lightweight media indexing.
Explored tradeoffs between compression ratio, latency, CPU usage, and operational complexity.
Maintained a habit of overthinking technical elegance before eventually learning when to ship.

volunteer.summary: Mentored early-career engineers and students interested in algorithms, backend systems, and startup engineering.
highlights: Helped students reason through algorithm design, debugging, and software architecture tradeoffs.
Reviewed small open-source projects and provided feedback on code clarity, tests, and maintainability.

awards.title: Startup Battlefield Winner
awards.summary: Recognized for a live technical demo of a compression platform built around the original Pied Piper algorithm.

awards.title: Emerging Founder Fellowship
awards.summary: Selected for early-stage technical leadership and product potential in infrastructure software.

publications.name: A Practical Approach to Lossless Compression for Media-Rich Applications
publications.summary: Outlined implementation tradeoffs in building a high-ratio compression system suitable for interactive media workflows.

publications.name: Compression, Latency, and Developer Experience in Distributed File Systems
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the Example Systems Workshop
publications.summary: Explored the relationship between compression performance, operational complexity, and developer-facing infrastructure design.

projects.name: Pied Piper Compression Engine
projects.description: A lossless compression engine designed for high-ratio media and file processing workloads.
highlights: Implemented core compression pipeline and benchmarking suite.
Optimized for compression ratio, latency, and memory usage across large file sets.
Built demos and developer tooling to explain performance tradeoffs.

projects.name: Pied Piper Distributed Storage
projects.description: A prototype decentralized storage platform using compression-aware chunking and replication.
highlights: Designed early architecture for file chunking, replication, and retrieval.
Explored trust, availability, and performance tradeoffs in peer-to-peer storage.
Built internal tools for observing storage behavior across simulated network conditions.

projects.name: Middle-Out Benchmark Suite
projects.description: A fictional benchmark suite for comparing compression techniques under awkwardly specific constraints.
highlights: Measured compression ratio, latency, CPU usage, and memory consumption.
Generated reproducible reports for algorithm comparisons.
Made technical demos slightly less chaotic.

references.name: References
references.reference: Available upon request, preferably after the demo works.

meta.note: This is fictional sample data for demonstrating a JSON Resume theme. It is inspired by a pop-culture software founder and is not intended to represent a real person.
